bullets.details: A Los Angeles jury found Meta and Google 70% and 30% liable, respectively, for designing addictive platform features that harmed youth mental health. This verdict, a first for such a case, creates a significant legal precedent for nationwide litigation.
bullets.topic: Meta and Google Found Liable in Landmark Youth Social Media Addiction Trial

bullets.details: A federal judge granted a preliminary injunction blocking the government's attempt to blacklist Anthropic, citing evidence of First Amendment retaliation after the company refused to grant the military unfettered access to its models.
bullets.topic: Judge Blocks Government Blacklist of Anthropic, Citing First Amendment Retaliation

bullets.details: Internal documents leaked from Anthropic reveal a highly capable new model, codenamed 'Claude Mythos' or 'Capybara,' which the company is testing under strict access controls due to concerns about its advanced coding and reasoning capabilities in cybersecurity contexts.
bullets.topic: Anthropic Confirms Testing 'Mythos' Model Following Major Data Leak

bullets.details: Starting April 24, GitHub will use interaction data from free, Pro, and Pro+ Copilot users for model training by default. Users must manually opt out via privacy settings to restrict this data usage; Business and Enterprise tiers remain exempt.
bullets.topic: GitHub to Use Copilot User Data for AI Model Training Starting April 24

bullets.details: Anthropic is reportedly discussing IPO plans for as early as Q4, with investment bankers estimating a potential valuation exceeding $60 billion.
bullets.topic: Anthropic Weighs Q4 IPO with $60B+ Valuation Target

bullets.details: Beijing-based AI startup Moonshot AI has reached a $2.5 billion valuation after securing $1 billion in new funding led by Alibaba and HongShan, underscoring continued investor appetite for Chinese generative AI.
bullets.topic: Moonshot AI Raises $1 Billion to Accelerate Generative AI Development

bullets.details: Mistral AI has launched Voxtral TTS, an open-source speech generation model optimized for edge devices and enterprise voice agents. The model features low latency and supports custom voice cloning with under five seconds of sample audio.
bullets.topic: Mistral Debuts Voxtral TTS for Enterprise Voice AI

bullets.details: English Wikipedia has officially prohibited the use of AI for writing or rewriting articles to protect content integrity. Editors are now restricted to using AI tools only for minor copy-editing and initial translation passes.
bullets.topic: English Wikipedia Bans AI-Generated Text in Articles

bullets.details: Meta has released TRIBE v2, a foundational AI model that simulates human neural activity. By serving as an in-silico digital twin for human brain response, the tool aims to accelerate neuroscience research and the treatment of neurological conditions.
bullets.topic: Meta Releases TRIBE v2 AI Model for Simulating Human Neural Activity

bullets.details: Chroma has released Context-1, a 20B parameter model designed for efficient multi-hop agentic search. It utilizes a novel self-editing mechanism to prune irrelevant context mid-search, enabling high performance at a lower token cost.
bullets.topic: Chroma Releases Context-1 Search Agent for Efficient Retrieval

digest_summary: A landmark LA jury verdict has held Meta and Google liable for youth social media addiction, marking a major legal precedent. Meanwhile, Anthropic faces a busy week: a federal judge blocked government blacklisting efforts, the company is eyeing a $60B+ IPO, and internal leaks confirm testing of a powerful new "Mythos" model. Elsewhere, GitHub will begin training its AI on user data starting April 24, English Wikipedia has banned AI-generated text, and advancements continue with new tools from Meta (TRIBE v2), Mistral (Voxtral TTS), and Chroma. In global news, Beijing’s Moonshot AI secured a $1 billion funding round.
digest_title: Meta and Google Found Liable for Youth Addiction; Anthropic Eyes IPO Amid Legal Wins
